messages.content: You must return JSON only. ALL fields in the schema must be present, even if null or empty.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a 20-year-old full-time student in Bologna, I've often found myself scrolling through social media, only to come across fake content that's not only annoying but also potentially misleading. For instance, I recently saw a post on Facebook claiming that a popular brand was offering discounts on their products. Sounds too good to be true, right? That's because it was. The post was completely fabricated, and when I dug deeper, I realized that it was just a clever way to get people to click on the link and generate advertising revenue. This got me thinking - how can we, as social media users, avoid falling prey to such scams and identify the real deal from the fake? First and foremost, it's essential to be critical of what we see online. If a post looks too good (or bad) to be true, it probably is. Don't click on suspicious links, and never share information without verifying it. Another crucial step is to stay informed about the latest social media trends and scams. Follow reputable sources, such as fact-checking websites and social media literacy initiatives, to stay up-to-date on the latest fake content circulating online. Lastly, don't be afraid to report suspicious content to the social media platform. Most platforms have a reporting feature that allows you to flag content that's not in line with their community guidelines. By taking these steps, we can all do our part in fighting fake content and promoting a healthier online community. In my opinion, this is a pressing issue that requires our collective attention. By being more mindful and critical of what we see online, we can all contribute to a safer and more authentic social media experience. This is a topic that affects us all, regardless of our age, background, or social media usage. We must all work together to create a social media environment that's free from fake content and promotes honest and respectful communication. As someone who's passionate about staying informed and up-to-date, I believe it's our responsibility to educate ourselves and others on how to spot and avoid fake content. By doing so, we can all contribute to a more positive and trustworthy online community.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I started by recalling a personal experience where I encountered fake content on social media. I realized that it was a clever way to generate advertising revenue and that I had fallen for it. I then thought about how to avoid similar situations in the future and came up with three essential steps: being critical of what we see online, staying informed about the latest social media trends and scams, and reporting suspicious content to the social media platform. Finally, I concluded that fighting fake content requires our collective attention and that we must all work together to create a healthier online community.
